title: Return explicit MeshCore errors for unsupported operations
description: Why this exists: MeshCore app clients must fail gracefully when they request features LICHEN does not implement or intentionally does differently. What needs to be done: enumerate unsupported operations such as MeshCore RF/channel/routing features that do not map to LICHEN, remote admin operations, store-and-forward, telemetry/location/time injection if not supported, firmware update flows, and any MeshCore-specific mesh operations. Implement explicit error/no-op responses rather than silent drops. Acceptance criteria: unsupported command table is recorded; tests cover representative unsupported requests; client receives deterministic error or harmless read-only response; docs can consume the same matrix.
notes:
Input from project-LICHEN-9c4y.13 command matrix: unsupported-operation tests should cover remote/admin/login/status/binary/anon/control/raw flows, MeshCore RF/path/flood/repeater commands, private-key/signing/factory-reset/reboot, raw packet send 0x41, and reserved 0x2c..0x31/0x35. Expected default is RESP_CODE_ERR + ERR_CODE_UNSUPPORTED_CMD unless matrix names NOT_FOUND/ILLEGAL_ARG/DISABLED.
Review correction from project-LICHEN-9c4y.13: unsupported-operation tests must assert CMD_SEND_SELF_ADVERT (0x07) returns RESP_CODE_ERR + ERR_CODE_UNSUPPORTED_CMD, not OK/no-op. Also add explicit out-of-range unknown command cases such as 0x00, 0x42, and 0xff returning ERR_UNSUPPORTED. ERR_CODE_FILE_IO_ERROR (5) should be reserved for tested compatibility-store failures, not general unsupported behavior.
